'''Troy Stone''' (born 13 December 1971) is an Australian [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a professional in [[England]] and Australia.

==Background==
Stone was born in [[Paddington, New South Wales|Paddington, New South Wales, Australia]].<ref>[http://misterfixit.tripod.com/profiles/stone_t.html Troy Stone] ''misterfixit.tripod.com''</ref>

== Playing career ==
A junior from Goulbourn United, Stone debuted in 1993 for the [[Cronulla Sharks]] as a centre.

However, in 1994, when Stone moved to join [[St. George Dragons|the Dragons]] he moved into the front row.<ref>Alan Whiticker/Glen Hudson: Encyclopedia of Rugby League Players, 1995. {{ISBN|1875169571}}</ref>
Stone played in the 1996 Grand Final for St George, which they lost to [[Manly Sea Eagles|Manly]]. Also in 1996, Stone played in his only representative game, playing for [[New South Wales City rugby league team|NSW City]] in their annual clash with [[New South Wales Country rugby league team|NSW Country]].

Stone then played the 1997 [[Super League (Australasia)|Super League season]] with the new [[Hunter Mariners]] franchise before joining the [[Canterbury Bulldogs]]. Stone played from the interchange bench for the [[Canterbury Bulldogs]] in their loss at the [[1998 NRL grand final]] to the [[Brisbane Broncos]].

Halfway through the [[2001 NRL season]], Stone joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]] after receiving little game time at Canterbury.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=6957 Troy Stone signs with Huddersfield] ''rleague.com'', 15 June 2001</ref>

Stone spent a season with the [[Widnes Vikings]] before retiring at the end of 2002.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20121105152445/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1G1-90858963.html Prop Stone picks wood] ''Daily Post'', 28 August 2002</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://thebulldogs.com.au/sportal.php?page=player&id=621|title=BULLDOGS RUGBY LEAGUE CLUB - OFFICIAL WEBSITE|website=thebulldogs.com.au}}</ref>
